# Guidance for installing the nidirect-frontend node package manager (npm) package
- For more information on npm, read the node.js article, [What is npm?](https://nodejs.org/en/knowledge/getting-started/npm/what-is-npm/)

## Before you install the nidirect-frontend package
You will need to:
- Install [node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/)
- Install [govuk-frontend npm package version 5.0.0](https://www.npmjs.com/package/govuk-frontend/v/5.0.0) or above.  
  For guidance on how to do this, visit the [GOV.UK frontend website](https://frontend.design-system.service.gov.uk/installing-with-npm/#requirements).
- Install a SASS compiler to generate the CSS code for both packages.  
  Most popular integrated development environments (IDE) support plugins or extensions that allow you to compile SASS easily. Or if you prefer, you can compile the SASS manually using the command line.

> **Note:** nidirect-frontend uses the modern Sass module system (`@use` and `@forward`). This requires Dart Sass 1.23.0 or above. The legacy `@import` syntax is no longer supported.

## Installing the nidirect-frontend package
1. Open a command line window
2. Navigate to root folder of your application using the change directory command `cd` followed by the path to the root folder of your application.

   For example: `cd user/documents/myapplication`

3. Within your application's root folder, install the npm package using the command:

   `npm i nidirect-frontend`

4. When the installation finishes, the nidirect-frontend package will be in the same folder that the govuk-frontend package is held.

## Implementing the code in your project
1. Check that both the govuk-frontend and nidirect-frontend packages are in your project's `node_modules` folder.

2. Compile the provided entry point file `nidirect-all.scss` using your SASS compiler. You must pass `node_modules` as a load path so the compiler can resolve both packages. For example, using the Dart Sass command line tool from your project's root folder:

   `sass --load-path=./node_modules node_modules/nidirect-frontend/nidirect-all.scss assets/css/main.css`

   If you are using the sass npm package rather than the global Dart Sass CLI, replace `sass` with `node node_modules/sass/sass.js`.

3. Link the compiled CSS file in the `<head>` of your HTML pages:

   `<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/main.css">`

## Customising SASS variables
The `nidirect-all.scss` entry point configures govuk-frontend's font family to Arial as part of the NIDirect brand. If you need to override other govuk-frontend settings, create your own entry point SCSS file rather than editing `nidirect-all.scss` directly. Changes to files inside `node_modules` will be lost when the package is updated.

In your own entry point file, load govuk-frontend using `@use` with a `with` block before loading nidirect-frontend:

```scss
@use "govuk-frontend/dist/govuk/index" with (
  $govuk-font-family: (Arial, sans-serif),
  $govuk-brand-colour: #0a78cd
);

@use "nidirect-frontend/layout/global-overrides";
@use "nidirect-frontend/base/colours";
@use "nidirect-frontend/layout/footer";
@use "nidirect-frontend/layout/header";
@use "nidirect-frontend/layout/snippets";
@use "nidirect-frontend/layout/miscellaneous";
```

> **Important:** govuk-frontend settings can only be configured at `@use` time. You cannot override them after the module has been loaded.
